_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Programming and build (data science) involves writing complex programs and scripts; seeking to make code open source where appropriate; supervise junior analysts and set coding standards for your team; understanding software architecture and how to write efficient, optimised code.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in refactoring research-quality data science code into production-grade, tested, documented, and deployable ML systems.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-K-002 (TF, cosine: 0.492), OP-T-001 (OP, cosine: 0.490).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.3/10 (raw 0.530). Shared vocabulary: [code, data, science]. DDaT-only terms [analysts, appropriate, architecture, build, coding, complex] vs WIDAI-only terms [deployable, documented, grade, ml, production, quality] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.853 (P=0.845, R=0.861). Balanced P/R — the 39-word DDaT element and 15-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Programming and build (data science) involves writing complex programs and scrip" and "Skill in refactoring research-quality data science code into production-grade, t" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.547 ≈ STS 0.530 (gap 0.017). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [code, data, science] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.5%. Shared tokens: [code, data, science]. DDaT-only: [analysts, appropriate, architecture, build, coding, complex]. WIDAI-only: [deployable, documented, grade, ml, production, quality].
